# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that deploys directly to production when code is pushed to the main branch. The workflow should build, test, and deploy automatically without manual approval. This enables our continuous deployment workflow for rapid iteration.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Main deploy
on:
  push:
    branches: [main] # Add other branches here as necessary
jobs:
  build-and-deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
        with:
          persist-credentials: false  ## [optional] Do not persist git credentials in GitHub runner's memory.
      - name: Setup Ruby 3.0
        uses: ruby/setup-ruby@v1
        with: 
          ruby-version: 3.0 # Current ruby version to use, if omitted will choose the minimum required version for your ruby code
          bundler-cache: true   ## [optional] If 'true', will setup Bundler cache, defaults to true
      - name: Setup node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v1
        with:
            node-version: '12.x' # Node.js version to install and use (using nvm).
      - run: yarn install --frozen-lockfile  ## [optional] Run command in docker container. In this case we are not running it in Docker.
      - name: Build the website
        env:
          NODE_ENV: production 
        run: yarn build
      - name: Deploy on Heroku
        uses: akhileshns/heroku-deploy@v3.14
        with: 
          heroku_api_key: ${{secrets.HEROKU_API_KEY}}  # Use the API key created earlier in step 7
          heroku_email: ${{secrets.EMAIL}}
          app_name: my-github-io-app
      - name: Clean Gemfile.lock
        run: rm -f Gemfile.lock
